slides.title: Simple Login System
slides.content:
# Putting It All Together

We'll create three pages:

1. `login.php` – form to submit username/password.
2. `dashboard.php` – protected page, only accessible if logged in.
3. `logout.php` – destroy session and redirect.

We'll use hardcoded credentials for simplicity.

slides.title: login.php
slides.codeSnippet: Login

slides.title: dashboard.php
slides.codeSnippet:
Welcome, !

This is your dashboard.

Logout

slides.title: logout.php
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
session_start();
$_SESSION = [];
if (ini_get("session.use_cookies")) {
    $params = session_get_cookie_params();
    setcookie(session_name(), '', time() - 42000,
        $params["path"], $params["domain"],
        $params["secure"], $params["httponly"]
    );
}
session_destroy();
header('Location: login.php');
exit;
?>

quizData.question: In the login example, how does dashboard.php know the user is logged in?
quizData.explanation: The login script sets $_SESSION['logged_in'] = true, and dashboard.php checks that.
